docs.content:
# Accenture Tech Summer Analyst 2026 — Project Context

**Owner:** Yohannes Nigusse
**Project established:** April 28, 2026
**Time horizon:** April 2026 → May 2027 (acceptance → internship → return offer → graduation)

---

## Purpose of This Document

This is the foundational context document for the Accenture project. Any new conversation in this project should be able to read this document and immediately understand:
- Who I am and what my situation is
- What I'm working toward and on what timeline
- Who the people are in this process
- What's already been decided
- What's still open
- What outside-Accenture factors shape decisions inside Accenture

If you're a fresh assistant reading this for the first time, **start here.**

---

## 1. Personal Snapshot

**Name:** Yohannes Nigusse
**Pronouns:** He/him
**Date of birth / age:** 21
**Nationality:** Ethiopian
**Current residence:** South Laurel, Maryland (with family)
**Permanent residence during school year:** St. Cloud, Minnesota
**Phone:** 571-564-8375
**Email:** [primary email — to be filled in by Yohannes]

### Education

- **Currently:** Junior at **St. Cloud State University (SCSU)**
  - Double major: **Computer Science + Economics**
  - Expected graduation: **May 2027**
  - Current GPA: 3.39 at SCSU (transferred in)
- **Previously:** University of Maryland Baltimore County (UMBC), 2023–2024
  - GPA at UMBC: 3.79
  - Pre-Computer Engineering, then CS
  - Transferred to SCSU for financial reasons (out-of-state F-1 tuition was unsustainable; SCSU $13K/yr vs UMBC $31K/yr)

### Immigration / Work Authorization

- **F-1 student visa** (primary status)
- **TPS-based EAD** for Ethiopia (current secondary work authorization)
  - Auto-extended through **July 1, 2026** by federal court order in *African Communities Together v. Noem* (D. Mass.), per USCIS I-9 Central guidance dated April 13, 2026
  - The litigation is ongoing; SCOTUS ruling on related Haiti/Syria cases expected late June 2026
- **Decision for the Accenture internship:** Use **F-1 CPT (Curricular Practical Training)** through SCSU's Center for International Studies as the primary work authorization basis. CPT does not depend on TPS or any court order. Treat TPS-EAD as backup only.
- See separate document: *Ethiopia TPS Strategy for Accenture Summer 2026* for full plan.

---

## 2. The Accenture Offer — What's Confirmed

### Role

- **Position:** Tech Summer Analyst
- **Company:** Accenture (consulting / professional services)
- **Office:** **Seattle, Washington** (locked in; SF was an alternate but cohort placements were already set)
- **Internship dates:** **July 8, 2026 → August 18, 2026** (~6 weeks)
- **Status:** Verbal offer accepted on phone; written offer pending arrival "anytime next week" (early May 2026)

### Compensation (estimated, awaiting written confirmation)

- Hourly: ~$32–35/hr (industry-standard reported rates for Accenture summer analysts in 2024–2025)
- Hours: ~40 hrs/week
- Gross pay over 6 weeks: ~$8,400–9,000
- Sign-on bonus: ~$2,000 (typical for Seattle cohort, per Levels.fyi reports)
- Relocation/housing stipend: TBD (likely ~$2,000 for Seattle based on prior cohorts)
- Total estimated package: ~$11,000–13,000 gross

### After-Tax Reality

- Federal tax: ~10%
- FICA: 7.65% (TPS workers are not FICA-exempt unlike pure F-1)
- Washington State tax: **0%** (one of the financial advantages of Seattle)
- Estimated net: ~$8,000–9,500 in pocket after taxes
- Estimated rent in Seattle for 6 weeks: ~$3,000–5,000 depending on housing choice
- Estimated walk-away amount: ~$4,000–6,000

### Decision Timeline

- Verbal offer received: April 28, 2026
- Written offer expected: ~May 1–5, 2026
- Decision response window: ~7 days from written offer (typical Accenture range)
- Background check: Begins after written offer accepted
- I-9 / E-Verify: Completed at start date (July 8, 2026)

---

## 3. People in the Process

### At Accenture

| Name | Role | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Kaila Gopal** | Recruiter, Atlanta — Early Careers Talent Delivery | Made the offer call April 28, 2026. Now my primary point of contact. Email: kaila.gopal@accenture.com / 678-657-5050. |
| **Erika L. Myrthil** | Recruiter, Dallas — Early Careers Talent Delivery | Original recruiter who scheduled both interviews. Handed me off to Kaila for offer processing. Still in network. |
| **Chandan Shah** | Strengths & Skills interviewer | Manager, 20-year Accenture tenure, Test Automation lead in Pune. Got Agentic AI certification in January 2026. Interview went well. |
| **Ajay Gupta** | Showcase & Scenarios interviewer (actual) | Salesforce Practice Architect, Agentforce-certified. Originally was the assigned interviewer; was supposed to be replaced by Vaughn but then conducted the interview anyway after Vaughn no-showed. Interview went well. |
| **Vaughn Parker** | Showcase & Scenarios interviewer (assigned, did not attend) | Quality Engineering Program Manager, Philadelphia. Was supposed to interview me, didn't show. Connected with me on LinkedIn after the fact. Soft signal, ambiguous meaning. |
| **Jill Standish** | Accenture Global Retail Lead | Quoted publicly in the Profitmind investment announcement. Referenced in my Showcase pitch. Don't know personally; relevant context. |

### Outside Accenture — In My Pipeline

| Name | Affiliation | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **Joselyn Vasquez** | Accepted Accenture ATC offer (Columbus) | Peer who helped me prep for the interviews. Best contact for "what's it like at Accenture" questions. Offered to keep helping. Strong relationship to maintain. |
| **Danaya** | Accepted Security TDP offer | Peer who advised that interview impact > technical depth. |
| **Nick Tsororos** | Morgan Stanley — Head of Tech Recruiting Globally | Connected on LinkedIn at NSBE 2026. Screening completed March 20, awaiting final response. Status open. |
| **Kieron I. + Lois Mruz** | Morgan Stanley screening team | Conducted my March 20 screening. |
| **Volaree Rendon, Rod English** | Bentley | Submitted 4-doc package; awaiting response. |
| **Various** | NVIDIA, Apple, GE Aerospace, Deloitte | NSBE 2026 contacts. Lower-priority follow-ups. |

### Personal Support Network

- **Family:** Currently living with family in South Laurel, MD during off-school months
- **SCSU NSBE chapter:** Strong peer support
- **SCSU Center for International Studies (CIS):** Critical for CPT processing — primary contact for work authorization
- **SCSU academic advisors:** Herberger Business School (HBS) advisor for internship course registration

---

## 4. The Story That Got Me Here

### Background Arc

- **2023:** Started at UMBC. CWIT scholarship recipient. Built Python automation for attendance tracking.
- **2023-2024:** SI PASS tutoring (Calc I & II), 10 months, 5-25 students per cohort.
- **August 2024:** Transferred to SCSU for financial reasons. First semester rough — got a C in Operating Systems (currently retaking).
- **Jan-May 2025:** BCI Lab research at SCSU (cold-emailed the professor). Joined NSBE chapter.
- **2025-2026:** Built personal AI engineer roadmap. Self-directed learning across RAG, agentic systems, infrastructure.
- **January 2026:** Started Kibur CO-OP — sole developer building AI admissions assistant for a college in Addis Ababa. Remote.
- **March 2026:** Built AI Aggregator end-to-end in 7 days for NSBE 2026 demo. End-to-end agentic content pipeline.
- **March 2026:** NSBE 2026 in Baltimore. Aggregator demo led to Morgan Stanley screening (March 20) AND Accenture interviews.
- **April 24, 2026:** Final round at Accenture (NSBE pipeline).
- **April 28, 2026:** Offer call from Kaila.

### What I Pitched (For Reference)

**Topic:** Agentic AI for mid-market QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) workforce scheduling
**Framing:** Same architectural pattern Accenture invested in via Profitmind, applied to scheduling instead of pricing/inventory
**Architecture:** Four-agent system
- Agent 01: Demand Forecaster (Prophet + LightGBM)
- Agent 02: Compliance Validator (deterministic rule code, NOT LLM — Fair Workweek hallucination = lawsuit)
- Agent 03: Schedule Optimizer (OR-Tools MIP — "same kind of math FedEx uses to optimize delivery routes")
- Agent 04: Manager Recommender (LangGraph orchestrator + contextual bandit, primary LLM)
**Stack:** MCP + LangGraph + AWS Bedrock (Deputy 2025 reference architecture)
**Personal hook:** I've worked both sides of this — associate manager at a bar-lounge in DC, currently working overnight shifts at a QSR near campus. Three days before the interview, I was sent home from my shift because we were overstaffed — sparked the pitch.

### Story Bank — 11 Stories I've Built Around

1. **AI Aggregator** (March 2026, 7-day solo build) ⭐
2. **Freelancing Water Company** (current, consulting-shaped) ⭐
3. **Personal AI Engineer Roadmap** (2025-ongoing) ⭐
4. **BCI Lab** (Jan-May 2025, cold-outreach to SCSU professor)
5. **Kibur CO-OP — AI Admissions Assistant** (Jan-May 2026, current) ⭐
6. **SI PASS Leader** (Aug 2023-May 2024, Math/Calc I & II) ⭐
7. **CWIT Events Assistant** (Jul 2023-May 2024) ⭐
8. **BeteSeb Graduation Committee President** (22 months, COVID pivot)
9. **UMBC → SCSU Transfer** (Aug 2024, financial decision) ⭐
10. **CS 310 Operating Systems** (C first time, retaking spring 2026) ⭐
11. **NSBE 2026 Convention** (March, Aggregator demo → Morgan Stanley + Accenture interviews) ⭐

---

## 5. Anchor Phrases I've Built (For Future Reference)

These are my voice, refined through interview prep. Use them as reference when writing new content in my style.

- *"When the standard path doesn't give me what I need, I go get it myself."* (taking initiative)
- *"Same content, different entry point."* (technical vs non-technical communication)
- *"The math doesn't change — my explanation does."* (SI PASS lesson)
- *"I tend to over-optimize before committing."* (real weakness, not the perfectionist cliché)
- *"What I'm doing on a small scale, Accenture is doing at enormous scale."* (why Accenture)
- *"CS is how you build systems. Econ is how systems work. Applied AI is where they meet."* (degree thesis)
- *"I'd rather miss the deadline clean than meet it by breaking a rule I can't undo."* (judgment under pressure)
- *"The pattern travels."* (about market expansion thinking)
- *"It turns the system from a scheduler into a live operations brain."* (scaling vision)

---

## 6. Outside-Accenture Context That Shapes Decisions Inside Accenture

### School (SCSU)

- **Spring 2026 (current):** Retaking CS 310 Operating Systems. On track for an A this time. Other coursework on track.
- **Summer 2026:** Internship at Accenture Seattle (July 8 – August 18). Academic break otherwise.
- **Fall 2026:** Senior year, fall semester. Full course load. Recruiting season for full-time roles begins.
- **Spring 2027:** Final semester. Graduation May 2027.
- **Critical decision point:** Whether Accenture's full-time return offer (likely arriving October-November 2026) becomes my post-graduation path, or whether I pursue other options.

### Financial

- Currently: F-1 student tuition at SCSU (~$13K/yr). Living at home in Maryland during summer breaks (no rent during summer).
- Internship summer (2026): Seattle housing for ~6 weeks. Estimated upfront cost ~$1,500-2,000 before first paycheck. Net savings from internship after housing: ~$4,000-6,000.
- Post-graduation (May 2027): Need either (a) Accenture full-time conversion or (b) separate full-time offer. F-1 OPT becomes available. STEM OPT extends that to 3 years total.

### Family

- Living at home with family in South Laurel, MD during summer 2025 prep period and during school breaks
- Family supportive of consulting career path
- No major family-related constraints on summer 2026 plan

### Other Recruiting Pipeline (Active)

- **Morgan Stanley:** Screening completed March 20, 2026. Awaiting response. If they offer something competing, the timing could affect Accenture decision (though Accenture is locked).
- **Bentley:** 4-doc package submitted. Status unclear.
- **NSBE 2026 contacts:** Various tier-2 conversations, low priority now that Accenture is locked.

### Decision Heuristic (For Competing Offers)

- Accenture is the offer in hand. Don't lose it chasing speculation.
- If a competing offer arrives, evaluate honestly on: long-term career fit, learning trajectory, network value, brand value.
- Don't compare on dollars alone — internship pay is roughly equivalent across these firms.
- Loyalty matters: Accenture extended the offer first; declining for a marginal alternative would be unwise unless the alternative is materially better.

---

## 7. What's Still Open

### Immediate (next 1-2 weeks)

- [ ] Receive written offer from Kaila
- [ ] Review written offer details (especially relocation stipend, exact start date, sign-on)
- [ ] Sign offer letter
- [ ] Begin background check process
- [ ] Email SCSU CIS to start CPT advising appointment
- [ ] Schedule immigration attorney consult (ILCM or AILA-Minnesota)
- [ ] Build TPS documentation file (USCIS pages, court order, etc.)

### Mid-term (May-June 2026)

- [ ] Identify SCSU internship course in HBS catalog for CPT registration
- [ ] Submit CPT application package to CIS (offer letter required)
- [ ] Receive CPT-authorized I-20
- [ ] Watch for USCIS successor I-9 alert before July 1
- [ ] Send transparency email to Kaila about CPT-as-primary work auth basis
- [ ] Begin housing search in Seattle (flexible/cancellable preferred)
- [ ] Plan logistics: flights, housing, local transit, banking

### During Internship (July-August 2026)

- [ ] Complete Form I-9 on July 8 with CPT-endorsed I-20 + passport (primary), TPS-EAD as backup
- [ ] Make a strong impression in the 10-week (6-week for me) cohort
- [ ] Build relationships with Seattle office team
- [ ] Connect with at least 2-3 senior consultants for mentorship
- [ ] Document my work for the return-offer evaluation
- [ ] Final week: ask about return offer process and timing

### Post-Internship (Fall 2026 — Spring 2027)

- [ ] Receive return offer (typical timing: October-November 2026)
- [ ] Decide on accepting return offer vs. other paths
- [ ] If accepting: prepare for May 2027 full-time start
- [ ] If not: continue full-time recruiting through fall semester
- [ ] Final senior year coursework
- [ ] Graduation May 2027

---

## 8. Communication Patterns I Use

### Tone

- Plain English over jargon
- Concrete over abstract
- "Same content, different entry point" mentality — adjust depth to audience
- Honest about what I don't know
- Calm in pressure moments

### Email Style

- Warm but not over-eager
- Specific subject lines
- Short paragraphs
- Clear asks and timelines
- Sign-off: "Best, Yohannes" (standard)

### Written Communication With Recruiters

- Match their tone and energy level
- Always reply within 24 hours during active conversations
- Reply to threads rather than starting new ones (keeps context)
- Be specific about times, dates, locations

### LinkedIn

- Accept connections from interviewers and recruiters
- Send brief, warm acceptance notes when relevant
- Don't ask about status through LinkedIn (use email/phone)
- Maintain peer relationships (Joselyn, Danaya, etc.) over time, not transactionally

---

## 9. What I've Decided

These are decisions already made. Don't re-litigate them in new conversations.

- **Accepting the Accenture Tech Summer Analyst offer** (verbally accepted; written acceptance pending offer letter)
- **Seattle is the office** (cohort placement locked, can't change)
- **Using F-1 CPT as primary work authorization** for the internship (TPS-EAD as backup)
- **Engaging an immigration attorney** for the CPT/TPS strategy (consult, not full retainer)
- **Treating Joselyn as a long-term peer relationship** (she helped me get here; stay in touch)
- **Treating Erika as a maintained relationship** despite handoff to Kaila (still in my network)
- **Not bringing up the Vaughn-Ajay interview mix-up** with Kaila or anyone else (process moved forward; no upside to raising it)
- **Not raising concerns about TPS legal status proactively with HR beyond the planned transparency email** (don't create a problem they don't see)
- **Pitching consulting as my primary career path** post-graduation (CS + Econ + AI = applied consulting)
- **Treating this internship as a long interview for full-time conversion** (the goal is to convert)

---

## 10. What I Still Need to Decide

These are open questions. Future conversations should help work through them.

- **Whether to file a Form I-765 (TPS-EAD renewal) speculatively** — pending attorney advice
- **Housing strategy in Seattle** — sublet, corporate housing, Airbnb, traditional lease?
- **Whether to maintain active applications with Morgan Stanley, Bentley, etc.** during the internship — or pause those pipelines and focus on Accenture conversion?
- **What to ask for during the internship to maximize learning** — projects, mentorship, exposure?
- **Whether to apply to AI Refinery / Microsoft FDE / Profitmind teams specifically** within Accenture during the internship — or stay general?
- **What full-time role to target post-graduation if I do convert at Accenture** — Tech Analyst, Strategy & Consulting, AI/Data?
- **Whether to pursue a Master's degree (CS, Econ, MBA) eventually** — or commit to industry track?

---

## 11. The Critical Documents

These are the documents I'm working from. Future conversations should reference these where relevant.

| Document | Location | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **Interview prep HTML (97 questions)** | /mnt/user-data/outputs/interview_prep.html | Final-round prep tool, used April 24 |
| **Showcase deck HTML** | /mnt/user-data/outputs/showcase_deck.html | The pitch I delivered |
| **Story bank** | /mnt/user-data/outputs/story_bank.md | 11 stories, full STAR format |
| **Accenture research archive** | /mnt/user-data/outputs/accenture_research_archive.md | Research on Accenture's strategy, products, recent news |
| **Kaila call cheat sheet** | /mnt/user-data/outputs/kaila_call_cheatsheet.md | Used for the April 28 offer call |
| **Ethiopia TPS strategy doc** | (artifact from prior session) | Full CPT-as-primary plan with attorneys, scenarios, timeline |
| **This project context document** | (current) | The foundation for all future Accenture project conversations |

---

## 12. How to Use This Project

### When starting a new conversation in this project:

1. **Read this document first** before responding to any question
2. **Check the "What's Decided" section** before suggesting alternatives
3. **Check the "What's Open" section** for legitimate areas to explore
4. **Use the "Anchor Phrases"** when writing in my voice
5. **Reference the "Critical Documents"** when relevant rather than rebuilding context
6. **Update this document** when major decisions are made or major events happen

### When I bring up something new:

- If it relates to a person already in this document, reference what I already have on them
- If it's a new person, ask me to add them to Section 3
- If it changes a decision, ask me whether to update Section 9
- If it's a new open question, suggest adding it to Section 10

### What this project is NOT for:

- General career advice unrelated to Accenture
- Coursework help unrelated to the internship pathway
- Personal life decisions outside the professional arc
- Other recruiting pipelines (those have their own context)

---

## 13. The Through-Line

If you forget everything else in this document, remember this:

I'm a junior CS + Econ student at SCSU, originally from Ethiopia, who built my way into Accenture through self-directed projects and a strong NSBE 2026 showing. I just received my Tech Summer Analyst offer for Seattle (July 8 – August 18, 2026). I'm using F-1 CPT as my primary work authorization. The goal is to perform well enough during the summer to convert to a full-time offer for May 2027 post-graduation. Everything in this project supports that arc.

The work between now and May 2027 is:
1. **Get to the start line clean** (offer signed, work auth squared away, housing set)
2. **Make the most of the 6 weeks** (real contribution, real relationships, real learning)
3. **Convert** (return offer, then decide)

That's the whole project.
